questions.q: Who wields the Sudarshana Chakra in the Mahabharata?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is Krishna's discus, the signature weapon of Vishnu.

questions.q: What kind of weapon is the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: It is a razor-edged spinning disc that flies at its target and returns.

questions.q: Which king did Krishna behead with the chakra at the Rajasuya assembly?
questions.explanation: When Shishupala's insults crossed the promised hundred, Krishna released the chakra and beheaded him.

questions.q: On which finger is the chakra classically shown spinning?
questions.explanation: Iconography shows the discus whirling on Vishnu's or Krishna's raised index finger.

questions.q: What happens to the chakra after it strikes its target?
options: It buries itself in the earth
It must be retrieved by a devotee
It returns to the wielder's hand
questions.explanation: Like a divine boomerang, the Sudarshana returns to Krishna after every cast.

questions.q: The Sudarshana Chakra is the emblematic weapon of which great god?
questions.explanation: Vishnu bears the chakra in one of his four hands, and Krishna carries it as his avatar.

questions.q: Roughly what does the name 'Sudarshana' mean?
questions.explanation: Su-darshana means fair or auspicious of vision — beautiful to behold, terrible to face.

questions.q: How many insults had Krishna vowed to forgive Shishupala before the chakra flew?
questions.explanation: Krishna had promised Shishupala's mother to pardon a hundred offenses — the hundred-and-first was fatal.

questions.q: At which grand ceremony of Yudhishthira's did the Shishupala episode occur?
questions.explanation: Shishupala erupted when Krishna received the first honor at Yudhishthira's Rajasuya sacrifice.

questions.q: Which city's lord, Krishna, kept the chakra through the Kurukshetra War without using it to fight?
questions.explanation: Krishna of Dwarka had vowed to bear no arms in the war, serving only as charioteer and guide.

questions.q: According to the Mahabharata, who gave Krishna the Sudarshana Chakra?
options: Agni, at the burning of Khandava
questions.explanation: In the Adi Parva's Khandava episode, Agni rewards Krishna with the celestial discus.

questions.q: Along with the chakra, which weapon did Krishna receive at the Khandava episode?
questions.explanation: Krishna received the mace Kaumodaki together with the discus in the Khandava reward.

questions.q: During the war, Krishna famously rushed at Bhishma in fury, weapon in hand. Per the Sanskrit epic, what did he raise?
questions.explanation: In the Bhishma Parva, Krishna takes up his discus against Bhishma (on a later day, only a whip); the chariot-wheel image belongs to later retellings and art.

questions.q: Why did Krishna abandon his charge at Bhishma without striking?
options: Arjuna restrained him and renewed his own vow to fight
Bhishma's celestial armor repelled the chakra
questions.explanation: Arjuna clung to Krishna and begged him to keep his no-arms vow, promising to fight in earnest.

questions.q: How did Bhishma respond when Krishna rushed at him with weapon raised?
options: He welcomed death at Krishna's hands as glory
He shot the weapon from Krishna's grasp
questions.explanation: Bhishma lowered his bow and hailed Krishna, saying death from him would be his honor.

questions.q: In the popular account of Jayadratha's death, what role is the chakra given?
options: It shielded Arjuna's chariot from arrows
It veiled the sun to create a false sunset
It cut down Jayadratha's six guardians
questions.explanation: Later retellings say the discus masked the sun; the critical text attributes the darkness to Krishna's power more generally.

questions.q: Which enemy of Krishna, the king of Chedi, was the chakra's most famous Mahabharata victim?
questions.explanation: Shishupala, the Chedi king and Krishna's cousin, fell to the discus at the Rajasuya.

questions.q: What had Krishna promised Shishupala's mother that delayed his punishment?
options: Never to raise arms in Chedi
To crown her son a king first
To wait until Shishupala struck first
questions.explanation: Krishna's vow to his aunt to forgive a hundred insults explains his long restraint.

questions.q: In popular tradition, what is said of Krishna's finger when he released the chakra at Shishupala?
options: The chakra's edge cut it, and Draupadi bound it
It turned the color of gold
questions.explanation: A beloved tradition says the discus nicked Krishna's finger and Draupadi tore her sari to bind it, earning her his protection.

questions.q: Beyond a weapon, what does the spinning chakra commonly symbolize?
options: The wheel of time and cosmic order
The unity of the four varnas
questions.explanation: The discus is widely read as kala-chakra, the wheel of time upholding dharma.

questions.q: Which epithet of Krishna directly references the discus he bears?
questions.explanation: Chakradhari and Chakrapani both name Krishna as the bearer of the discus.

questions.q: Which flying city did Krishna destroy with the chakra, as he recounts to Yudhishthira in the epic?
questions.explanation: Krishna recounts destroying Salva's aerial city Saubha, finishing the battle with his discus.

questions.q: In Puranic tradition, from what did Vishvakarma fashion the Sudarshana Chakra?
options: The churned essence of the ocean
questions.explanation: The Puranas say Vishvakarma ground down the sun's excess radiance and forged the discus from it.

questions.q: What relation was Shishupala to Krishna, deepening the tragedy of his death?
options: His paternal cousin, son of his aunt
questions.explanation: Shishupala was the son of Krishna's father's sister, making the execution a kinsman's duty.

questions.q: What happened to Shishupala's spirit when the chakra slew him, as the epic describes?
options: It wandered as a ghost over Chedi
It entered the body of his son
A flame from his body merged into Krishna
It was dragged to Yama's court
questions.explanation: Onlookers saw a light leave Shishupala and dissolve into Krishna, a sign of liberation by the Lord's hand.

questions.q: In the tale of the false sunset, why did Krishna intervene against the natural day?
options: To save Arjuna from his fire-vow against Jayadratha
To let the Pandavas retreat unseen
To fulfill a promise to Subhadra
questions.explanation: Arjuna had sworn to die by fire if Jayadratha lived past sunset; the feigned dusk drew Jayadratha out.

questions.q: Which weapon-class does the Sudarshana belong to among traditional astra-shastra categories?
options: A shastra, since it never leaves the hand
An astra-like hurled weapon that obeys its master's will
A siege engine of the gods
questions.explanation: Hurled yet returning and guided by divine will, the chakra is counted among the supreme launched weapons.

questions.q: How many spokes is the Sudarshana traditionally described with in later iconographic texts?
questions.explanation: Later tradition often gives the discus 108 serrated edges or spokes, a sacred number.

questions.q: Why did Krishna NOT use the Sudarshana to end the Kurukshetra War quickly?
options: He had vowed to remain weaponless in the war
questions.explanation: Krishna pledged to both sides he would not fight; his near-use against Bhishma is the famous exception that proves the vow.

questions.q: In a celebrated Puranic tale, Vishnu wins the chakra from which god by offering a thousand lotuses, giving his own eye as the thousandth?
questions.explanation: When one lotus fell short, Vishnu offered his own lotus-like eye, and a pleased Shiva bestowed the Sudarshana on him.
